creator: chris@netmonger.net
text:
FreeBSD makes more extensive use of OpenSSL now.  I wonder if those
who originally didn't want this file in the base system will have a
different opinion now.  Perhaps they didn't understand that it's
a standard feature of OpenSSL.  It's kind of like not installing
/usr/share/tmac.

I also point out the existence of /usr/src/crypto/openssl/certs/,
which does not appear to get installed.  If OpenSSL were built and
installed by hand, these files would be installed.  When it become
part of the FreeBSD build, however, certificate verification was
effectively "broken".

I am able to verify Thawte-signed cerficates using either your file or
the certs directory from OpenSSL.
